name: m16.3-b4-rocprof-megakernel-vs-production-selected
harness: scripts/mtp_verifier_rocprof.py --backend hip_gfx1100 --chain-attn-mode batched --decode-tokens 8 --candidate-budget 3 (full trace, 6 verify passes)
finding: Megakernel = 216.9 us/call (32 blocks) vs production selected FFN = rotate1 4.8 + gate_up_dual 41.7 + silu_rotate_down_staged 35.2 = 81.6 us/call (two wide GPU-filling kernels). Megakernel is 2.66x SLOWER on the GPU; kernel_ms/pass 13.8->19.2 (+5.4 ms/pass). The microbench's own fused number (~205 us at krot=8) matches rocprof (217 us) -- the microbench was right about the megakernel but compared it against an 8x strawman (naive non-staged chain ~678 us, not the deployed 81.6 us).
root_cause: Occupancy starvation: 32 blocks (rows) on 48 CUs leaves >=16 CUs idle, and the gate_up stage uses only 64/256 threads (64 ffn-packs). The production path parallelizes each GEMV over rows x output-packs (2048 / 8192 work items) and fills the GPU. To use >32 CUs the kernel MUST split output columns across blocks -- which fights the single-launch on-chip-intermediate fusion.
